cards.back: A Datagram is a self-contained packet of data in a network communication that is delivered as soon as it is available, regardless of whether it reaches its destination or not
Distance Vector Routing (DVR) is a type of unicast routing protocol used to find the shortest path between nodes in a network
Link-State Routing (LSR) is a type of unicast routing protocol used to determine the shortest path between nodes in a network by maintaining a map of the network topology

Path Vector Routing (PVR) is a type of unicast routing protocol used to determine the shortest path between nodes in a network by maintaining a list of paths from the source node to the destination node
Routing Information Protocol (RIP) is a simple unicast routing protocol used to find the shortest path between nodes in a network, commonly used in small networks
